mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding economic activity and rising corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or average forward returns are not positive when INDPRO is above the previous 12-month median.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained GDP expansion, reflected in rising quarterly real output, lifts aggregate corporate profits and supports equity valuations over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Correlation between GDPC1 year-over-year change and forward 60-day returns is less than 0.2 over the past 10 years, or direction is negative in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4%, the labor market becomes extremely tight, putting upward pressure on wages and eroding corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns over the ensuing month.
failure_condition: Average forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE < 4%, or t-test shows no significant difference from periods with higher unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate rises above 2%, the monetary stance becomes restrictive, compressing valuation multiples and reducing equity performance over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year win rate for negative forward returns when FEDFUNDS > 2% is below 60%, or Rank IC stays below -0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising consumer prices and unemployment define a stagflationary environment that depresses both earnings growth and risk appetite, producing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return is positive or zero in months that satisfy both trigger conditions over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-interest-rate environment (FEDFUNDS below 2%), rising housing starts signal robust residential investment and consumer confidence, driving positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS < 2%, the correlation between HOUST monthly change and 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Large monthly increases in nonfarm payrolls (>200,000) signal strong economic momentum, boosting short-term investor sentiment and producing positive equity returns over the following five trading days.
failure_condition: Average forward 5-day return is zero or negative in the week following payroll releases with MoM gains above 200k, or hit rate below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is elevated (above 110) alongside very low unemployment (below 4%), the economy is overheating, prompting margin compression and eventual mean reversion, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Average forward 60-day return is positive over all quarters that satisfy both conditions in the available sample, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is negative.
